title: Whole genome amplification of dengue virus type 1 - 4
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7f4nu","text":"Dengue virus is one of the most often imported tropical viral infection in Hungary. For next-generation whole genome sequencing of dengue virus serotypes 1 - 4, a one-step reverse transcription PCR assay was developed. PCR amplicons can be used for further amplicon based sequencing protocols on Illumina MiSeq platform. The genome of the dengue virus is a single-stranded, positive-sense, capped RNA of approximately 10‚Äď11 kb in length. The whole genome amplification was carried out by five overlapping PCR amplicons, each one is approximately 2500 nucleotide in length. For ampification of the four serotypes, four different primer sets were designed by Geneious Prime (version 2021.2.2) primer design tool. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Nucleic acid extraction: Use Qiagen QIAamp Viral RNA Mini Kit (cat. no. 52904 or 52906) for nucleic acid extraction. Nucleic acid extraction should be done by following the manufacturer's instructions. The total volume of the extracted viral RNA is 60 ¬Ķl.

Reagent name: Invitrogen‚ĄĘ SuperScript‚ĄĘ III One-Step RT-PCR System with Platinum‚ĄĘTaqHigh Fidelity DNA Polymerase (cat. no. 12574030 or 12574035) Keep all components, reaction mixes, and samples on ice. After preparation of the samples, transfer them to the preheated thermal cycler and immediately start the RT‚ÄďPCR program. Dengue virus whole genome can be amplified by 5 overlapping fragments for each sample. Detailed description of primer sets for the whole genome amplification of DENV serotypes 1 - 4 can be found in the Excel sheet (Primers_DENV_whole_genome_amplification) attached below. Primers_DENV_whole_genome_amplification.xlsx PCR setup: Add the following to a 0.2‚ÄďmL, nuclease-free, thin-walled PCR tube on ice: A B 1 Components Volume (¬Ķl) for 1 x rxn 2 SuperScript‚ĄĘ III RT/ Platinum‚ĄĘ Taq High Fidelity Enzyme Mix 0.5 3 2X Reaction Mix (a buffer containing 0.4 mM of each dNTP, 2.4 mM MgSO4) 12.5 4 Autoclaved distilled water 5.0 5 Sense primer (10 őľM) 1.0 6 Anti-sense primer (10 őľM) 1.0 7 Total volume 20.0 8 Template RNA (1 pg to 1 őľg) 5.0 9 Final volume 25.0 PCR master mix components for 25.0 ¬Ķl reactions. For multiple reactions, you can prepare a master mix to minimize reagent loss and enable accurate pipetting.

Program the thermal cycler so that cDNA synthesis is followed immediately with PCR amplification automatically: A B C D 1 Reverse transcription 55¬įC 30 mins 1x 2 Activation/initial denaturation 94¬įC 2 mins 1x 3 Amplification 94¬įC 15 sec 40x 4 53¬įC 30 sec 5 68¬įC 4 mins 30 sec 6 Final extension 68¬įC 5 mins 1x 7 HOLD 12¬įC infinite Cycling conditions for whole genome amplification of dengue virus type 1 - 4 Gently mix and make sure that all the components are at the bottom of the amplification tube. Centrifuge briefly if needed. Place the reaction tubes in the preheated thermal cycler programmed as described above.
Agarose gel electrophoresis: for visualization and interpretation of the results 1% agarose gel should be used.
creator: Anna Nagy
